title: --env doesn't work with variable name alone
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

[--env](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html#env-e-env) does not work with *only* a variable name.  ie `-e VAR_NAME` works in docker, but not podman, `-e "VAR_NAME=$VAR_NAME"` works in both

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `export VAR_NAME=whatever`
2. Run container with `-e VAR_NAME`

**Describe the results you received:**

$VAR_NAME is not set in the container

**Describe the results you expected:**

$VAR_NAME should be set in the container

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.2-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.13
Built:        Tue Jun  8 03:44:00 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

No, but I have asked on IRC and mheon confirmed and asked for me to file a bug

comments.body:
So sorry, this is not a podman bug.

Long story short I was running `sudo podman` aliased to docker, and sudo doesn't pass env variables by default :facepalm:
